prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene was computed using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding method) via xtb in the gas phase. The neutral ground state (charge 0, multiplicity 1) and the cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states all converged successfully. The most electrophilic site is the iron center with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (atom 11), and the most nucleophilic site is also the iron center with f⁻ = 0.25938191. Charge conservation checks passed for both f⁺ and f⁻ (sums = 1.0000). The analysis indicates that the iron center dominates the Fukui reactivity, with the cyclopentadienyl ligands showing substantially lower reactivity indices.
